title: URL Space Traps, Structural Fingerprints, Soft Errors, and Stop Evidence · Go Build Room
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds trap detection and bounded containment policy from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of trap detection and bounded containment policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Detect temporal, pagination, facet, search, session, nonce, sort, repeated-segment, depth, and parameter-combination expansion.
Bound distinct path templates, query keys, values, combinations, depths, and pages per class with owned exceptions.
Combine status, redirect, title, main text, structure, known markers, and repetition to classify soft error and challenge pages.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Trap Features
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 4866 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to contain calendar, query, session, structure, and soft-error traps with bounded incident evidence; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Trap Features.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect temporal, pagination, facet, search, session, nonce, sort, repeated-segment, depth, and parameter-combination expansion. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Trap Features, keep Trap Features connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Trap Features preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Only exact duplicate links cause crawl loops. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Trap Features.

For commit to the mission model, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Trap Features for trap detection and bounded containment policy.

stimulus.title: Containment Incident case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A workshop Go team handles case 6485 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to contain calendar, query, session, structure, and soft-error traps with bounded incident evidence; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Competency probe: Pause smallest affected scope, retain trigger and state counts, notify owner, preserve partial evidence, and require restart approval.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Skipping trap-looking URLs silently is safe and auditable.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pause smallest affected scope, retain trigger and state counts, notify owner, preserve partial evidence, and require restart approval. Artifact under review: trap detection and bounded containment policy.

options.text: The Containment Incident record says trap detection and bounded containment policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the initial evidence packet evidence boundary for Containment Incident.

For audit the initial evidence packet, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Containment Incident for trap detection and bounded containment policy.
The record names the assumption, applies Containment Incident, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
